```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  fa <- sample(seq(0.05,0.45,0.05),1); fb <- sample(seq(fa+0.10,0.95,0.05),1)
  ans <- fb-fa; vals <- round(c(ans,fb,fa,1-ans,fa+fb),3)
  if(length(unique(vals))==5L) break
}
a <- sample(-3:1,1); b <- a+sample(1:4,1); opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Para uma variável aleatória contínua, $F_X(`r a`)=`r fa`$ e $F_X(`r b`)=`r fb`$. Qual é $P(`r a`<X\leq`r b`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
$$P(a<X\leq b)=F_X(b)-F_X(a)
=`r fb`-`r fa`=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S5Q2_intervalo_pela_FDA
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
